documentation.readme: ESP-IDF is the development framework for Espressif SoCs supported on Windows, Linux and macOS.
usage_sections: Please read the support policy and the documentation for more information about ESP-IDF versions.
Please see the End-of-Life Advisories for information about ESP-IDF releases with discontinued support.
Install host build dependencies mentioned in the Getting Started guide.
Run the install script to set up the build environment. The options include install.bat or install.ps1 for Windows, and install.sh or install.fish for Unix shells.
Run the export script on Windows (export.bat) or source it on Unix (source export.sh) in every shell environment before using ESP-IDF.

arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with espressif/arduino-esp32 (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with zynon/LdapRelayScan (score=9); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with zodiacon/HookHunter (score=9); pair in the chain planner.
Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 4: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 5: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the wireless_ble_ext category. Fits when the engagement needs: ESP-IDF is the development framework for Espressif SoCs supported on Windows, Linux and macOS.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: ESP-IDF is the development framework for Espressif SoCs supported on Windows, Linux and macOS.
documentation.how_to_use: Clone or install `esp-idf` from its upstream repository, then run `esp-idf --help` (or the README entrypoint) to confirm flags. ESP-IDF is the development framework for Espressif SoCs supported on Windows, Linux and macOS.

name: esp-idf
summary: ESP-IDF is the development framework for Espressif SoCs supported on Windows, Linux and macOS.

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need wireless_ble_ext, use esp-idf because ESP-IDF is the development framework for Espressif SoCs supported on Windows, Linux and macOS.
When chaining wireless_ble_ext tools, pair esp-idf with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `esp-idf --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
